topic: OpenAI Codex CLI Guide 2026: Terminal AI Coding with the New Rust-Built Agent

key_points: Installation via npm (npm i -g @openai/codex) or Homebrew
Authentication with ChatGPT account or API key
Built in Rust, open source under Apache 2.0
Supports macOS, Windows, and Linux
Describes plain-English task instructions and file editing
Complete guide covering installation, setup, and advanced usage
Covers approval modes (on-request, workspace-write, --yolo)
AGENTS.md custom instructions for project context
MCP server integration for third-party tools
Subagents for parallel task execution
Highlights Rust architecture for speed and security
OS-enforced sandboxing: Apple Seatbelt (macOS), Landlock/seccomp (Linux)
Compares Codex CLI vs Claude Code autonomy philosophy
GPT-5.4 model with up to 1M token context
Full-auto mode vs supervised modes
Setup walkthrough and hidden features
Config file at ~/.codex/config.toml
Project-scoped .codex/config.toml for trusted projects
Skills system for reusable task templates
Integration with VS Code and Desktop app via shared config
Claude Code vs Codex CLI feature-by-feature comparison
Codex uses GPT-5.4/GPT-5.3-Codex; Claude Code uses Claude models
Codex leans toward unsupervised autonomy; Claude Code toward supervised
Codex free tier available; Claude Code requires Anthropic subscription
Both support MCP and terminal-first workflows

stats.fact: OpenAI Codex CLI reached 3 million weekly active users as of April 8, 2026 (confirmed by Sam Altman)
74,468+ GitHub stars (third after Claude Code at 112,297 and Gemini CLI at 100,857)
32.8 million total npm downloads over the past 12 months; downloads nearly tripled in one month coinciding with GPT-5.4 release
640+ tagged releases shipped (roughly one per day since launch), 5,075+ commits, 400+ contributors, 9,000+ forks
GPT-5.4 supports 272K default context window, configurable up to 1M tokens
Codex CLI included in ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, Edu, and Enterprise plans at no extra cost
Growth from 600,000 weekly active users at start of 2026 to 3 million in three months

angles: Complete beginner-to-advanced setup guide: npm install, auth, first task in under 5 minutes
Security-first angle: OS-level sandboxing (Apple Seatbelt / Landlock) vs app-layer hooks in other tools
Approval modes deep-dive: when to use workspace-write vs on-request vs --yolo flag
AGENTS.md and config.toml workflow for reproducible project-level AI behavior
Subagents for parallelizing complex multi-file refactors — practical examples
MCP integration: connecting Codex CLI to databases, APIs, and third-party services
Free-tier access via ChatGPT subscription vs API key pricing comparison
Real-world workflow: combining Codex CLI with Git checkpoints for safe experimentation
